119707.explanation: We, the residents, govern our own municipality. When an issue is highly pressing, a referendum can help make a decision. But preferably, we involve residents well before a decision through participation in local democracy.

119710.explanation: This is the ideal picture: residents jointly produce their own green and affordable electricity with a wind turbine or solar panels. They use the electricity onsite, so the grid is not burdened. Energy cooperatives are an asset to our municipality and should be encouraged.

119714.explanation: Banning completely means you must also enforce it. In practice, that is difficult. At the same time, wood burning does cause environmental pollution and health complaints. We prefer education and campaigns encouraging people to be considerate of each other.

119716.explanation: Residents can bring their bulky waste to the municipal yard for free. There it is well separated. If collected for free at home, everything gets mixed and ends up with residual waste. Processing that is expensive, which means higher waste charges for our residents.

119721.explanation: Local media are important to know what is happening in your own area, from the street to the municipal council. We are satisfied with how things are going now. Extra funding does not seem necessary.

119722.explanation: In Montfoort and Linschoten, we are fortunate that all school buildings have already been improved or newly built.

119723.explanation: In Montfoort and Linschoten, children can already receive subsidies if parents cannot afford music lessons (Youth Fund Sports and Culture). Fortunately, we have that well arranged.

119726.explanation: Appointing more municipal enforcement officers (boa's) is very costly. Progressive Agreement advocates that the boa's we have be better deployed and more visible on the street. That works preventively and avoids ticket frustration.

119727.explanation: It is very important to feel safe on the street. But we do not think more cameras are always the right solution. Your privacy is a great asset that should not be infringed lightly. We do find cameras helpful for license plate registration to prevent shortcut traffic through the city center.

119728.explanation: If you no longer feel safe at home, there must be a place you can always go to. A women's shelter is indispensable in those cases. But Montfoort is too small to offer enough privacy and protection. A solution must be found together with neighboring municipalities in the region.

119731.explanation: Holiday homes are not a permanent solution to the housing shortage. Montfoort does not have many holiday homes, so that hardly helps against the housing shortage. The holiday homes that exist also make our municipality attractive for tourists, which is important too.

119732.explanation: We support the expansion locations De Bleek and Park Rapijnen. Our municipality is already building a lot outside village boundaries. With plans at Doeldijk and De Goederen, the village feeling is already under pressure. We must also protect the green and cultural historical character of our landscape well.

119734.explanation: If the waiver of municipal taxes stops, people with the lowest incomes in our municipality will face bigger problems. They simply cannot miss that money. We believe everyone should be able to keep their head above water; therefore, we are in favor of waivers.

119735.explanation: This statement is difficult to answer properly. It depends too much on specific situations. Besides a low income, other problems may also play a role. Appropriate help is always a matter of tailoring.

119737.explanation: Electric driving is the future. It fits municipal policy to become more sustainable so that more people leave the fossil car behind and choose to drive on green electricity. Lack of charging stations must not stand in the way.

119739.explanation: It is important to be who or what you are, also when choosing healthcare. At the same time, it is valuable if we as humans do not only choose our own 'bubble' but also remain open to those who think differently.

119740.explanation: The waiting lists and problems in youth assistance and youth care are enormous. You do not solve that by cutting more budgets and always choosing the cheapest option. We must provide certainty and help tailored to the concerns of our youth. Cheap can sometimes be expensive.

119741.explanation: This statement is too one-sided. Paying less tax sounds nice, but then as a municipality you must be honest about which tasks you will no longer perform or from which other budget you will take the money. Property tax is not levied for fun, but to finance useful services.

119744.explanation: Montfoort has a strong mix of agriculture, manufacturing, and crafts. This business activity is economically and culturally indispensable. We want to preserve and strengthen it, fitting the Montfoort scale. The municipality must support this with appropriate space, accessible sites, and sustainable growth.

119746.explanation: We basically agree with this statement, but that applies just as much to other target groups dependent on priority. It is not the group you belong to that determines your priority, but how urgent your situation is: who needs a roof over their head the most.

119748.explanation: A field of solar panels can provide many households with clean, affordable energy. If farmers want to, they should be able to use their land for this. Moreover, solar panels can be well combined with more biodiversity, for example by letting flowers and other low greenery grow.

119752.explanation: The agreement is that 30% of the homes in Montfoort must be social rental housing. But because too little has been done for years, it is now only 16%. This means little supply for residents who depend on it. We want more social rental homes; that helps residents and other target groups.

119753.explanation: Many homes urgently need to be built. If you don’t want to take from the green outskirts again, you have to build upwards. But too tall obviously doesn't fit our rural and village character. So 'high apartment buildings' in Montfoort terms means about four floors.

119755.explanation: The historic centers of Montfoort and Linschoten were not built for today's cars. Within a radius of a few hundred meters, parking is available everywhere. Car-free shopping streets mean more peace for residents, safety for cyclists and pedestrians, and shoppers stay longer.

119756.explanation: That ship has sailed, whether you agree or not. If Montfoort does not cooperate, the province will install them. Then it’s better to be in control yourself to get the most sustainable, least landscape-intrusive, and financially most advantageous result for your residents.

119759.explanation: There’s always a lot going on around the IJssel bridge in Montfoort center. The bridge keeper’s house has been renovated with much volunteer effort. That the bridge is operated remotely doesn’t make it any less lively. Especially when the volunteer fire brigade cools down the hot bridge deck in summer.

119761.explanation: Linschoten’s center is too valuable to sacrifice for parking spaces. There should not be more, but smarter parking. And that’s possible. It’s not the residents, but the restaurant visitors who cause the congestion. You can manage that smarter too. Just outside the center, there is plenty of space.

119707.explanation: Referendums often provide an oversimplified representation of reality. The CDA supports a sensible weighing of pros and cons.

119710.explanation: The CDA finds it important that sustainability initiatives are supported.

119716.explanation: 'Free' does not exist. The CDA considers a good municipal yard important.

119722.explanation: The municipality builds reserves to invest where necessary in the quality of schools.

119723.explanation: There is a Youth Fund for low-income families and the municipality offers subsidies for both structural and occasional activities in the municipality.

119727.explanation: This can occasionally be a solution for safety issues.

119728.explanation: Montfoort continues to support regional facilities.

119731.explanation: The CDA supports building sufficient homes and maintaining a vital recreation sector.

119732.explanation: The CDA finds it important that enough homes are built for Montfoort and Linschoten residents.

119734.explanation: The CDA wants a constructive approach to financial problems.

119735.explanation: The CDA wants constructive assistance for people with financial challenges.

119737.explanation: The CDA supports facilitating electric driving.

119739.explanation: Because of high costs, reimbursement policies have not existed since 2025.

119740.explanation: Quality of care is a very important aspect for the CDA.

119741.explanation: Reducing property tax would cause large budget gaps.

119744.explanation: The CDA finds it important that an attractive business climate remains in the municipality of Montfoort.

119746.explanation: The CDA finds it important that Montfoort and Linschoten residents can continue living in our municipality.

119752.explanation: For new construction, we apply 1/3 social rental, 1/3 affordable ownership, and 1/3 middle/high segment.

119753.explanation: The CDA supports preserving our municipality’s rural character.

119755.explanation: The CDA supports a car-reduced city center.

119756.explanation: The placement of wind turbines by the Province near Papekop did not proceed according to agreements. The CDA sent a letter to the Province about this. Resistance to wind energy at Cattenbroek causes us to lose control over the revenues to the Province.

119761.explanation: For the CDA, safe passage for emergency services and preserving the village character are priorities. Residents, hospitality businesses, and churches must be involved in parking policy development.

119707.explanation: Decisions should be made by the elected municipal council. They weigh interests and take responsibility. Referendums often oversimplify complex topics and do not provide a structural solution.

119710.explanation: Sustainability initiatives are important, but not all projects need to be supported with municipal funds. The municipality must handle taxpayers’ money carefully and not take financial risks on behalf of residents.

119714.explanation: Using fireplaces and wood stoves should remain a personal choice.

119716.explanation: Free bulky waste collection makes it easier for everyone to dispose of waste responsibly. This keeps the municipality clean and livable for all residents.

119721.explanation: The local broadcaster already receives subsidies. Extra money is not necessary; the municipality must use taxpayers’ money carefully and not structurally invest extra in a single organization.

119722.explanation: School buildings have been recently renewed and extra investments are not needed now. It is important, however, to have a good multi-year maintenance plan to maintain quality in the long term.

119723.explanation: Music lessons are already offered in primary schools. It is not the municipality’s task to provide extra subsidies for this; schools can organize music education themselves.

119726.explanation: More enforcement officers ensure a safe and livable municipality. They can respond more quickly to nuisances and address problems so that residents feel safe.

119727.explanation: More camera surveillance helps prevent and solve nuisance and crime. This contributes to the safety and livability of the municipality.

119728.explanation: A women's shelter is important, but the municipality cannot organize this independently. It is a task for national or regional institutions; Montfoort can cooperate or support where possible.

119731.explanation: This is not an issue in Montfoort; holiday homes are not used as permanent residences here. Therefore, there is no reason to allow or prohibit this.

119732.explanation: New construction outside the borders may be necessary for housing or business opportunities. It is important that this is done carefully and fits with the environment and livability of the municipality.

119734.explanation: Forgiving municipal taxes costs the municipality a lot of money. Residents with low incomes are, of course, supported through existing national schemes, so the municipality handles tax money responsibly.

119735.explanation: The municipality already applies national income limits for poverty support. These are clear and appropriate; an increase is not necessary.

119737.explanation: In the city center, charging spots must not come at the expense of regular parking spaces. In residential areas, the charging spots should also serve as parking for electric cars, ensuring enough space remains for residents.

119739.explanation: Care must be accessible and of good quality for everyone. A choice for a specific religious belief or culture cannot always be guaranteed; more important is that care is professional and appropriate.

119740.explanation: When selecting organizations for youth assistance, both price and quality must be considered. This keeps help affordable and effective for the youth and the municipality.

119741.explanation: The property tax (ozb) may be lowered to keep housing affordable, but only if the municipality's financial situation permits. This ensures sufficient funds remain for important municipal tasks.

119744.explanation: The industrial estates in Montfoort and Linschoten may be expanded. This allows businesses to grow and maintains space for employment in the municipality.

119746.explanation: Residents of Montfoort must be given priority for social rental housing.

119748.explanation: Solar panels on very good agricultural land should not be allowed. This land is important for food production and must not be used for energy generation.

119752.explanation: We focus on as many owner-occupied homes as possible. Additionally, about 30% of new construction is social housing, which is sufficient for a varied housing supply.

119753.explanation: High apartment buildings do not fit the rural character of Montfoort and Linschoten. We choose low-rise buildings that suit the surroundings and preserve livability.

119755.explanation: The city center of Montfoort must be low-traffic to ensure it is pleasant to stay. At the same time, it remains important that the center is easily accessible for residents, visitors, and entrepreneurs.

119756.explanation: Wind turbines do not fit the landscape and character of the municipality.

119759.explanation: The IJssel bridge is managed by Rijkswaterstaat. Therefore, the municipality of Montfoort cannot determine how its operation is arranged.

119761.explanation: The historic character of Linschoten must be preserved. Therefore, no additional parking spaces will be created in the center, but they will be located on the outskirts so visitors can park well without affecting the center.

119707.explanation: It is important that the residents of Montfoort/Linschoten have direct influence on important decisions in the municipality.

119710.explanation: The municipality has limited financial resources which must be fairly distributed among all residents of Montfoort. It is not important to generate energy as this has no value.

119714.explanation: Residents may choose to use open fireplaces themselves.

119716.explanation: Collecting bulky waste will lead to a significant increase in costs, which will be passed on to all citizens, even those who have little or no bulky waste.

119721.explanation: The money made available will be allocated to the local broadcaster. They have a limited role in news provision due to social media.

119722.explanation: All school buildings in Montfoort/Linschoten have been recently renovated and, in our view, meet all required standards. If this is not the case, we would like to hear from the schools.

119723.explanation: With the introduction of the Groene Hart pass, activities that are free or discounted for children are already taken into account.

119726.explanation: Safety is the responsibility of the police and the public prosecutor, who must ensure security. Otherwise, deploying enforcement officers costs the municipality a lot of money.

119727.explanation: More camera surveillance promotes safety and helps combat vandalism, destruction, and drug trafficking.

119728.explanation: Municipality Montfoort is too small for a women's shelter. The safety of persons in the women's shelter cannot then be guaranteed.

119731.explanation: This helps reduce the pressure on housing shortages.

119732.explanation: To meet housing needs, houses must also be built outside the existing village boundaries.

119734.explanation: Agreements have been made with other municipalities that this area is handled uniformly everywhere and cannot be unilaterally canceled.

119735.explanation: Support should be specifically aimed at those most vulnerable to ensure effectiveness.

119737.explanation: Many electric parking spaces have been designated in Montfoort and are not all used. If there is demand for extra charging points for electric cars in a certain area, these can be created.

119739.explanation: It is desirable that care aligns as much as possible with personal beliefs, but this must not come at the expense of accessibility, quality, and affordability of care for everyone.

119740.explanation: The quality of care is important when assessing organizations for selecting youth assistance.

119741.explanation: The property tax (ozb) in Montfoort is much higher than in other municipalities and can easily be lowered. We submitted an amendment not to increase the ozb, but no party supported this.

119744.explanation: This promotes employment, which is very important for the Montfoort community, as well as for associations that are often supported by local entrepreneurs. Therefore, it is important that businesses can expand and do not have to move to another municipality.

119746.explanation: Housing seekers from the local municipality who have been registered much longer must be given priority.

119748.explanation: Five years ago, Montfoort made it possible to establish a solar park, but nothing has come of it. Now there is no longer any demand for it, nor for the electricity generated during summer daytime.

119752.explanation: In Montfoort, it was agreed that 30% of the homes should be social rental housing. The current council and coalition have decided that this did not need to be met in a certain construction project, which is unacceptable to us.

119753.explanation: The zoning plan prescribes the building height in the municipality of Montfoort. It may be possible to consider the old garden center location for a taller building, if desired.

119755.explanation: In the city center of Montfoort, cars are currently guests and this works well. The council unanimously decided to keep the traffic plan as it is. However, a motion by the coalition to investigate reversing the driving direction was adopted, which is now no longer possible.

119756.explanation: Wind turbines have significant consequences for public health. The farmers on whose land they are placed will be held liable for health effects and the depreciation of property values.

119759.explanation: This bridge has never been managed by the municipality of Montfoort, and we especially do not want to be assigned this problematic bridge because it will cost a lot of money.

119761.explanation: In Linschoten, it must be ensured that emergency services can reach all necessary locations. Residents were strongly opposed to the proposed parking plan. A new council must consult residents per area to determine needs. The current proposal does not meet legal requirements.

119707.explanation: We strive for a society where citizens are actively and constructively involved in shaping their own living environment. Whether a referendum is the right tool depends on the subject. Good communication with residents is crucial.

119710.explanation: We want to encourage sustainable energy generation. Under the right conditions and objectives, each project can be assessed to decide if the municipality wants to financially contribute. It is important that a project can only proceed with municipal support.

119714.explanation: We support clean air because it contributes to a healthy living environment. Therefore, emissions of fine particles and other harmful substances should be prevented as much as possible.

119716.explanation: This promotes efficient and environmentally friendly processing, contributing to the circular economy. Bulky waste should be viewed as raw materials to be reused whenever possible.

119721.explanation: Local broadcasting is important due to its social character. If it can no longer operate independently, the municipality could financially support it under the right conditions and objectives.

119722.explanation: Good housing for education is essential. Education shapes children and young people in who they are, how they approach life, and how they can contribute to society. In recent years, we have committed ourselves to good educational housing.

119723.explanation: Music is essential for cognitive skills and socio-emotional development. It also has a connecting effect.

119726.explanation: We advocate good enforcement of public order and safety by supervising, enforcing rules such as parking and waste regulations, tackling alcohol and nuisance. Neighborhood-focused work and good coordination with youth workers is important.

119727.explanation: The municipality should be cautious in this and only use it if it increases safety, reduces nuisance and vandalism— and then only in specific locations or during certain periods.

119728.explanation: If it contributes to the well-being of this specific group to be housed in smaller municipalities. As a municipality, we must also take responsibility by contributing to housing and welfare of this group.

119731.explanation: We support a dignified existence in a regular home and neighborhood. Prevention of potentially undesirable poor and unsafe living conditions. Prevention of landscape deterioration.

119732.explanation: The housing shortage is severe, so we prioritize those seeking housing. Developments should fit the landscape, not be too large-scale, and as much as possible be for residents of Montfoort and Linschoten. Preference for infill but also adjacent street expansions.

119734.explanation: Montfoort is a prosperous municipality. It is unacceptable that people live in poverty, with shame and shortages. Many health complaints stem from worries about daily living costs. Therefore, the municipality must contribute to preventing or reducing poverty as much as possible.

119735.explanation: Poverty affects families, singles, and children. The number of people living in poverty must drastically decrease, which requires solid poverty policy. It is unjust that in Montfoort there are workers who cannot or barely make ends meet.

119737.explanation: Use of clean electric cars should be encouraged because it ensures cleaner air. A good expansion plan should be developed based on the number of electric cars owned in the municipality, so charging points are added and available at the right time.

119739.explanation: Good care can only be provided if it is appropriate care, meaning it should align as much as possible with the identity of the care recipient.

119740.explanation: Youth care faces major challenges, such as the interests of providers, expectations of help seekers, and financial pressures from all sides. While finances are important, quality is much more important to ensure a good long-term solution.

119741.explanation: This should be balanced and depends on inflation.

119744.explanation: It is desirable to maintain or promote employment and livability of local centers. Local entrepreneurs take social responsibility and are involved in the community. Preference for circular companies with added value and attention to greenery in development.

119746.explanation: When there is a shortage of social rental homes, it is important to investigate who has the greatest need based on local demand and legislation. Assess neighborhood capacity. All priority groups must be treated equally according to national regulations.

119748.explanation: Generating solar energy contributes to a cleaner environment, provided it is not too large-scale. We give opportunities to citizen initiatives and support local energy communities. Residents share the benefits, so revenues serve the community.

119752.explanation: Currently, only 16% of homes are social rentals. Based on Christian-social values of solidarity and mercy, we want to commit to building social rental homes for starters, those with limited means, and people with care needs.

119753.explanation: Only if it fits the landscape and municipality character can one tall residential building be built to relieve housing pressure.

119755.explanation: Car-restricted, but everything must remain accessible for disabled people and the elderly, with attention to wide sidewalks and cyclists. Cycling should remain an attractive alternative to cars. A dense network of bike and pedestrian paths is necessary. Traffic safety is a priority.

119756.explanation: The foundation of our future energy supply is a proper mix of wind and solar energy and is important to contribute to a circular economy. Through local energy cooperatives, at least 50% ownership for residents, including those with limited incomes. Attention to health.

119759.explanation: Operation could be in-house if it improves service and is practically feasible with volunteers. It strengthens social cohesion and can be a valuable role for volunteers.

119761.explanation: Safety must be guaranteed for all road users. Preserve the historic village character. If these two important conditions are met, parking expansion is possible. More parking spaces are desirable.

119707.explanation: A referendum creates arbitrariness. The number of referenda can get out of hand. Municipal elections offer a way to express opinions. A citizen assembly would be preferable as it is proactive in decision-making rather than reactive.

119710.explanation: D66 supports stimulating sustainable energy, but this could lead to grid congestion. It is better to take action to reduce or manage grid congestion and actively assist in this.

119714.explanation: There is already too much fine particulate matter around Montfoort. We advocate clean air. Highly efficient wood stoves fit within a decent heating system. Open fireplaces can be replaced with efficient wood stoves.

119716.explanation: Provided on demand. The higher the threshold (people with low income, limited education, no mobility) to dispose of waste properly, the greater the chance it will end up in unwanted places in our environment.

119721.explanation: Free journalism is a great asset which we are happy to support as a municipality.

119722.explanation: Although many school buildings in Montfoort have already been renewed, it is important to always discuss potential improvements in consultation with school management.

119723.explanation: Not for all children, but in case of financial hardship, assistance can be requested from the municipality.

119726.explanation: At least three enforcement officers (boa's) are now necessary.

119727.explanation: Unless necessary due to increasing crime.

119728.explanation: If this is necessary within the region and preferably together with one or more neighboring municipalities.

119731.explanation: Provided these homes comply with building regulations.

119732.explanation: The housing shortage is very high, but first investigate housing construction locations within the municipal boundaries. Infill development is preferred.

119734.explanation: The municipality can check whether someone truly cannot contribute. Possibly make it income-dependent.

119735.explanation: Due to rising costs there is a lot of hidden poverty. Therefore, definitely keep up with inflation.

119737.explanation: Placement of charging stations must keep pace with the number of electric cars within the municipality. D66 monitors this critically and wants to prevent an increase in the scarcity of parking spaces.

119739.explanation: We must keep care affordable. Moreover, this would not promote integration. The demand for care is so great and often urgent that in allocation, faith, culture, and beliefs must play no role.

119740.explanation: Of course, a good price-quality ratio is important, with quality being more important than price.

119741.explanation: No need to lower it; increase only if necessary.

119744.explanation: Must not come at the expense of building land for homes. Only infilling on existing sites. Solve more regionally. No further urbanization of rural areas.

119746.explanation: As a municipality, we must comply with national legislation.

119748.explanation: We support sustainable energy, but given grid congestion it is unwise to place more solar panels on agricultural land. It is better to first use the roofs of businesses and buildings. More energy hubs must be created to use energy more effectively.

119752.explanation: Needed in the coming period to make up for lost ground.

119753.explanation: Montfoort is a rural municipality. High residential flats do not fit this image.

119755.explanation: This would benefit shopping and tourism. It is important that there are enough parking spaces around the center. Residents should be able to park near their home with a permit. Limit the number of permits per residence.

119756.explanation: The province has already decided that wind turbines will be built. Careful consideration is needed on how to reduce grid congestion so that we can use all generated power and new companies don't have to wait for a power connection. Local participation is essential.

119761.explanation: Parking spots outside the village center must be considered. More parking in the center of Linschoten does not fit the village view.

statements.title: Montfoort should oppose the installation of wind turbines.

titleParts.text: Montfoort moet zich verzetten tegen de komst van

titleParts.glossary: These are modern wind turbines that convert wind power into electricity and can be up to 150 meters high.

statements.title: The residents of Montfoort should be able to give their opinion on important issues via a referendum.

titleParts.text: De inwoners van Montfoort moeten over belangrijke onderwerpen hun mening kunnen geven via een

titleParts.glossary: A public consultation where residents can express their opinion on a political issue. The municipal council is not obliged to follow the result; legally it cannot.

statements.title: Montfoort should install more camera surveillance.

titleParts.glossary: Monitoring of a building, area, or (groups of) people using security cameras.

statements.title: The municipality should support the local broadcaster with additional funding.

titleParts.glossary: Radio and television by and for people from the municipality and the immediate area.

statements.title: The use of open fireplaces and wood stoves should remain permitted in Montfoort.

titleParts.text: In Montfoort moet het gebruik van open haarden en

titleParts.glossary: Wood-burning stoves used to heat a home.

statements.title: Residents who want to generate sustainable energy together should receive financial support from the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Energy based on resources that are effectively infinite, such as sunlight and wind.

titleParts.text: op willen wekken, moeten financiële steun krijgen van de gemeente.

statements.title: The municipality should prevent new construction outside existing village boundaries.
The municipality should invest money in improving all school buildings during the next council term.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet in de komende

titleParts.glossary: The municipal council is elected for a period of four years.

titleParts.text: geld steken in het verbeteren van alle schoolgebouwen.

statements.title: Montfoort should deploy more municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) who focus on safety.

titleParts.glossary: People who, for the municipality, check whether citizens and businesses comply with the rules.

titleParts.text: (boa’s) komen die zich met veiligheid bezighouden.

statements.title: At least 35% of new homes built in Montfoort should be social rental housing. Currently in the Netherlands 28% of homes are social rentals.

titleParts.text: Van de nieuw te bouwen woningen in Montfoort moet minimaal 35% een

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

titleParts.text: zijn. In Nederland is nu 28% van de woningen een

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: Bulky waste must be collected from homes free of charge.
Every primary school child should be able to receive music lessons subsidized by the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Money the government gives to citizens, businesses, and organizations to support them.

titleParts.text: van de gemeente muziekles kunnen krijgen.

statements.title: The municipality must allow holiday homes to be used as a permanent residence.
The municipality should stop forgiving municipal taxes for low-income residents.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet stoppen met het

titleParts.glossary: That people no longer have to pay for something.
Taxes you have to pay to the municipality (e.g., sewer charge, waste disposal charge, dog tax).

titleParts.text: voor inwoners met een laag inkomen.

statements.title: Montfoort should establish a women's shelter (domestic violence shelter).

titleParts.glossary: Shelter for women who experience violence from their partner or family. The shelter is secured; sometimes the location is secret.

statements.title: People who need care should always be able to choose a caregiver who matches their religion, culture, or beliefs.

titleParts.text: Mensen die zorg nodig hebben, moeten altijd kunnen kiezen voor een zorgverlener die past bij hun geloof, cultuur of

titleParts.glossary: What someone believes about life, such as a religion or personal conviction.

statements.title: The municipality should designate more parking spaces as charging points for electric cars.
Montfoort should take control of operating the IJssel Bridge.
The municipality should raise the income threshold for receiving poverty assistance.
When selecting organizations for youth care, the municipality should primarily pay attention to price.
The center of Montfoort should become car-free.
High-rise residential buildings may be allowed in Montfoort.
The property tax (onroerendezaakbelasting, OZB) should be reduced.

titleParts.glossary: Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.
Property tax. Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.

statements.title: The municipality must allow solar panels on agricultural land.
Industrial/business parks in Montfoort may be allowed to expand.
The municipality should give refugees with a residence permit priority for social housing.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet vluchtelingen met een

titleParts.glossary: The right to reside in the Netherlands. The term can refer to the right itself or to the card that shows someone has that right.
A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: The center of Linschoten should get more parking spaces.
